item.title: Never judge a book by its coveralls
item.description: Video originally posted to TikTok by user @mudbug_a.m.r. Much longer video there. The title of this post is a comment made by user Elora Danan. This is at the Edmonton Public Library. They were working next to it. Piece is J. S. Bach prelude No.2 in C Minor BMV 847 (arranged by Luo Ni)

item.title: Bus drivers
item.description: I always walked to school and I always walked my son to school so we never experienced a bus driver like these folks did. Unfortunately, I've read too many stories of bus drivers who were not so nice. I am focusing on the good ones here.

item.title: 50th Oodle of Unmuted Faves - Anniversary Edition
item.description:
I posted the first Unmuted Oodle almost exactly 3 years ago. Fuck me, time does really does fly. So for the 50th edition, I went through all the previous Oodles and picked out my 25 MOST favorite favorites. Enjoy!
Credit: https://twitter.com/Pandamoanimum/status/1526503971719888898
Jeff Arcuri https://www.jeffarcuri.com/
Live Collegiate Shag https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xYrTzBBTPnA Music: "E Doce Morrer No Mar" by Vignes Rooftop Revival https://rooftoprevival.com
Antonio Vivaldi "L’inverno" (Winter)
"No Man's Land (The Green Fields of France)", performed by: NatiDreddd https://www.youtube.com/user/NatiDreddd Seth Staton Watkins https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCLy--Ncf_wGvVCsHft5CMTw
Music: Charlie Puth - Betty Boop / Good Golly https://youtu.be/yAheUVmBtAY
Tony Ann, "My neighbor's car alarm" https://m.youtube.com/user/tonyannmusic/featured
Jen Hamilton https://www.instagram.com/_jen_hamilton_
K. S. Rhoads https://www.instagram.com/ksrhoads
Andrii Bondarenko https://instagram.com/andrii_bondarenko
The late, great Willie Spence https://www.youtube.com/@williespence4892
Ant & Dec https://www.antanddec.com/
Ukrainian pianist Alex Karpenko defiantly playing through air raid sirens outside a subway station in Lviv, March 2022. Artist: Alex Karpenko https://www.instagram.com/alexpian_official Footage: John Stanmeyer https://www.instagram.com/p/CbS-DD-q5oO/ Music: "Time" from the Inception score by Hans Zimmer ----- For more unmuted faves, just click on the #oodles tag - and ignore the counter that's been stuck somewhere around 29 for ages. It really is 50. Also, technically, one Oodle equals 25 clips (because I randomly said so in the very first one) but since the Hamilton one has two parts I decided it counts as one, so there. Also also, for anyone who started subscribing to this account back when I still had it in me to produce actual OC and has instead only been getting these Oodles I'm posting to fill the void: I'm sorry and I promise I'm trying to get my writing mojo back and there WILL eventually be a post about Nuts (or Not-Nuts). And more awesome cemeteries. <3

item.title: Saturday wholesome dump of smile crepes (that's a kind of a tasty pancake)
item.description:
1. You just know they are made for each other : )
2. Pigeon boxing
3. Ruuuuun
4. Heckin cute
5. Look at this innovative way of catching stuff
7. True king
8
9. When the tickle hits the right spot
10. This guys is in some awesome films!
11
12
13
14
15. Thanks for scrolling all the way trough! Hope this dump made you happy! Finally I would like to share with you a little passion project of mine. Me and my brother are making a game that is inspired by "The Stanley Parable" and the narrator definitely has a touch a of GladOS to him. The game is part of Next Fest 2024 and it would the mean the world to us if you wishlisted: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/

item.title: Polio and Me
item.description:
Edit: I’m absolutely overwhelmed with the warmth and kindness of everyone. I’ve been yearning for this kind of acceptance for 60 years. Imgur-ites thank you. So. Very. Much. My heart is full and I feel peaceful and calm. ♥️ ——- Long post - if you're up for reading and learning a little about polio - the gift that keeps on giving. (Not sure why I wrote it in the 3rd person). Whatever. TL:DR OP gets polio in Africa. Has abusive, cult-y evangelical parents. Fun times ensue. My USA parents were hard core evangelical missionaries (massive cringe). Let's call it what it was / is - a Cult. They went to Africa to "save brown souls". (Awful). I was born there in 1960. A year later I came down with polio. Was paralyzed from my neck down. Couldn't move my legs or torso. (Not me in the photo - photo credits below) https://www.npr.org/sections/health-shots/2012/10/16/162670836/wiping-out-polio-how-the-u-s-snuffed-out-a-killer
Couldn't breathe. Sent to hospital in Zimbabwe where I enjoyed living in an iron lung for several months while in the hospital. Their church had a massive worldwide prayer chain praying for a miracle to happen in the hospital. Like most polio survivors (unbeknownst to them), several months later, I magically was "healed". I was released from the hospital and removed from the iron lung. Mom and Dad and the rest of the church celebrated this miracle that their prayer chain and their god had made happen. As an aside, my parents were severely physically abusive. Lots of beatings. Slappings. We were neglected while they were out saving other souls. This happened my entire life - to all 4 of their children. As I grew up I was always the miracle child and held up as an example. I'd meet people from around the world as I was with my folks and they'd remember the call for prayer and would marvel at how I was healed so dramatically. Praise god. Little did they know that I was struggling to walk. That my feet and legs hurt horribly. That it was hard to breathe. I kept pretending. I had to. I didn't want to let everyone down. My feet were a mess. It was so hard to find shoes to fit. When we'd go to buy shoes my mom would regale the shoe salespersons with my miracle polio healing. (So F-in humiliating). I would fall down in the middle of the street for "no reason". I couldn't run. I was always walking way behind everyone else. I couldn't play sports. I'd double over gasping for breath - at home - at school. Everywhere. My parents didn't pay attention. Yay I was healed. They continued to talk and praise god for the miracle of my healing. Meanwhile they beat us mercilessly. But my dad was a minister so we all had to pretend everything was fine when we showed up for church. We came back to the US when I was 13. It became harder and harder to walk. My arches were incredibly high. My toes were curled up and twisted - they rubbed the tops of my shoes till they were raw. I continued to fall down for "no reason". "OP why'd you fall?" What could I say? I was a kid. And they abused us terribly, I certainly wasn't going to let them know they were telling a lie. The truth is I wasn't healed from polio. My polio had a lifecycle like most polio survivors. It had nothing - of course - to do with god. But it made for a good story. Praise god. Finally the pain was too much and OP told mom and dad. Off we went to the doctor who recommended surgery for the worst part - OP's knee cap was sliding all over creation. Incredibly painful surgery took place. Mom was thrilled to have another thing to talk about again - she got a lot of attention because her daughter, who had polio in Africa and who was healed - was now having to have surgery. (It makes no sense as I wouldn't have had to have surgery if I was healed.) But it made sense in OPs mom's head. So - mom started to push for more surgery. We visited doctors and she told her tale of polio and the incredible pain I was in. She made up stories about pain I was in and made me lie so I'd get more surgery. I could tell the doctors didn't believe her / me. But she was instient that I needed this surgery - the most extreme options always of course. In total I ended up having 5 separate surgeries on my knee and feet. Several of these I didn't need. At one point while I was trying to go to college she had me have surgery on both feet so I was in a wheelchair while trying to go to college. She loved it - so much attention for her. (Yes, it's called Munchausen syndrome by proxy) OP hated her mom and her dad (who kept beating all the kids until we left home). OP just wanted to get away from them damnit. OP grew up. Moved away from home (at 17) and away from the control of her abusive parents. She graduated from college, and almost graduated from Grad School. She got into software sales. She loved it. A way to make money to stay independent. She tried to ignore her weak feet and legs. She worked with doctors on her breathing. She adapted. She worked out and worked out and worked out hard to try to get strong. She continued to struggle to find shoes. She often had to stop on the street and hold onto buildings because she just couldn't walk anymore. But she kept going. She had to. She had to make money to live and stay independent. She founded a software company and did very well. She traveled all over. It got harder and harder to walk to clients, or through airports, or to carry her luggage, or to climb stairs to give speeches. She began to wonder what the hell is going on with my body? Am I just out of shape? Am I getting weaker? Why can't I do the exercises I used to do etc? She wondered if it had to do with polio. She visited a polio clinic in Boston where she was diagnosed with PPS - Post Polio Syndrome. Who knew? Apparently after 40, 50, 60 years many polio survivors begin having what feels like polio symptoms again. What has happened is that many motor neurons were destroyed when she had polio. Polio destroys 60% of these or more. And then, many of the remaining ones were destroyed by over exercising, over doing things, continuing to walk etc., even when it was painful. So she had a tiny amount of motor neurons left. And they don't regenerate. Motor neurons directly control all of our muscle movements. And she had a tiny amount left.  Read about motor neurons https://www.simplypsychology.org/motor-neuron.html# OP now uses a non-invasive ventilator for breathing at night (her diaphragm is incredibly weak). She sometimes walks with forearm crutches and sometimes uses a wheelchair when she's especially weak. She's afraid of killing her remaining motor neurons. She continues to fall a lot. That "healing" she got from god - was a total fraud. She always knew it, but had to go along with the lies and deceit. She was sacrificed so these nutmeg missionaries could feel good about their pretend god. This was a total mind F_ that landed her in therapy (among other things like PTSD and complex trauma) She pretended and endured operations she didn't need. To this day, her parents deny everything. To them, she was healed despite obvious evidence to the contrary. Just 2 years ago, she finally cut off her parents and the rest of her family completely. A difficult choice but ultimately absolutely the right thing to do. And OP is a happy and finally at peace, atheist. Doesn't have any belief in the pretend man in the sky. Very spiritual though. And finally peaceful. Thanks for listening. It has helped me to be able to write this and get my story out there to whomever reads it. And if there's anyone out there with Post Polio Syndrome - I'm happy to take questions. I'm pretty knowledgable at this point and can point people in the right direction.  For those that have made it this far, I thank you. XO Learn about Post Polio Syndrome https://polionetwork.org/encyclopedia
Me in Africa after I had miraculously healed from polio. You can't see my legs and feet but they are wonky.
Me a little older in Africa with my sister. Holding a flame lily. You'd never know my sister and I were being brutally abused. Thanks for reading XO

item.title: Wholesome post to brighten your day 1250
item.description:
https://imgur.com/gallery/7bXhXfY Wholesome Post 1249 https://imgur.com/gallery/0KxdbC9 Wholesome Post 1248 https://imgur.com/gallery/Osq2H5y Wholesome Post 1247 https://imgur.com/gallery/2J7nVrN Wholesome Post 1246 https://imgur.com/gallery/ZhZAXED Wholesome Post 1245 https://imgur.com/gallery/wQfxHPL Wholesome Post 1244 https://imgur.com/gallery/FHfLhAY Wholesome Post 1243
I include a Gofundme link in all of my posts to help raise money for people in need. I hope we can help https://www.gofundme.com/f/help-amanda-and-kerry
I made a wholesome support server on discord, whether you need support, would like to give it or just want to chat with some lovely people and look at some wholesome things it's a nice place. Here's the link if you'd like to join us. https://discord.gg/8M983spH48
I hope this post brightened your day :) and i hope it is a lovely day for you! If you need to talk I am always available on discord (username HaveAGreatDay) and happy to listen. You matter, and if you are struggling please reach out for help. I made a wholesome support server on discord, whether you need support, would like to give it or just want to chat with some lovely people and look at some wholesome things it's a nice place. Here's the link if you'd like to join us. https://discord.gg/8M983spH48

item.title: A defeated German soldier returns home from war to find nothing but the rubble of his home and his wife and children gone, Frankfurt, 1947. That is the reality of war.
item.dc:creator: Joyika

item.title: We Can Call Time of Death for 6,000 Year Old Religion ... Taylor Swift's new album draws criticism from Christian leaders who say it mocks God, Christians
item.description: Taylor Swift's new album, The Tortured Poets Department, has garnered criticism from faith leaders who have taken to social media this week to express their distaste with the lyrical content that they believe mocks God and Christians. Released last Friday, critics have voiced concern over lyrics that seem to suggest that Christians are both hateful and judgemental. In the song "But Daddy I Love Him," Swift sings: "But daddy I love him / I just learned these people only raise you / To cage you / Sarahs and Hannahs in their Sunday best / Clutchin' their pearls, sighing, 'What a mess' / I just learned these people try and save you 'Cause they hate you." Shane Pruitt, the National Next Gen director for the Southern Baptist Convention's North American Mission Board and co-author of Calling Out the Called, wrote in a Facebook post that there is a difference between being secular and being "Anti-Christian." He urged Christian parents to seriously consider if they should allow their children to listen to Swift's music. "I'm definitely not the minister or parent that has the 'no secular music' stance," Pruitt wrote. "Also, I fully realize unbelievers are going to act like unbelievers. HOWEVER, there is a difference between being secular and being ANTI-CHRISTIAN." Pruitt added that he used to listen to Swift but said he thinks "now it's time to reconsider." MovieGuide also objects to the lyrics of the song "Florida!!!," which the organization says is about "going off the deep end and all in on worldly fun and entertainment." "While Swift has since clarified that this song was about a fantasy rather than an actual experience she has had, it still glorifies going off the deep and engaging in complete debauchery," the organization states. Sean Feucht, a conservative Christian singer and former worship leader at Bethel Church who founded the Let Us Worship movement, took to X, formerly known as Twitter, to share how he believes the new album is making fun of Christians. "Almost half the songs on Taylor Swift's new album contain explicit lyrics (E), make fun of Christians and straight up blaspheme God. Is this the music you want your kids listening to?" Feucht wrote. And the COMMENTS are entertaining with a lot of finger pointing, Bible quotes and some actual discourse ... https://www.christianpost.com/news/taylor-swifts-new-album-mocks-god-christians-critics-say.html
item.dc:creator: Jbelkin
